notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 53 assertions vs. 34% baseline — the skill adds structural rigor that freeform analysis completely lacks

Baseline correctly identifies the right verdict (split/no-split) in all 4 cases — the skill's value is not in the decision but in the artifact quality, fidelity verification, and adversarial validation
With-skill uses ~2.3x more tokens and ~5.6x more time than baseline, reflecting the 4-phase protocol overhead
Eval 3 baseline proposed a 3-way split while the skill protocol constrains to binary split/no-split — both approaches have merit
Eval 1 with-skill used Mode A fallback (single-agent roles) instead of Mode B multi-agent dispatch — may indicate the adversarial integration could be improved
Eval 4 (learning loop) showed the biggest time difference (811s vs 100s) but also the biggest quality gap on planning gate specificity
Fidelity scores: 1.00, 1.00, 0.96, 0.952 — all above the 0.95 threshold, with Eval 3's lower score reflecting genuine boundary complexity
